channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Komani residents face electricity disconnections amid dispute over municipal charges and unpaid services
item.description: Residents of Enkululekweni in Komani, Eastern Cape, have been given until 27 April to settle outstanding municipal accounts or risk having their electricity purchases blocked by the Enoch Mgijima Loca...

item.title: Transport stakeholders push for fuel levy review amid rising bus fares in Tanzania
item.description: Transport stakeholders in Tanzania have raised concerns over increasing fuel-related levies and overall transport costs following a recent adjustment of bus fares by the Land Transport Regulatory Auth...

item.title: Delhi High Court rejects Kejriwal’s plea seeking judge’s recusal in excise policy case
item.description: In a significant development in the Delhi excise policy case, the Delhi High Court on April 21 refused to allow the recusal plea filed by former chief minister Arvind Kejriwal, Manish Sisodia, and oth...

item.title: An analysis of Bushra Bibi’s political influence and its limits in Pakistan
item.description: This opinion piece examines the complex and controversial public perception of former first lady Bushra Bibi and the nature of her influence in Pakistani politics. The article argues that much of the ...

item.title: Africa Soft Power Summit to explore integration of finance, creativity, and human capital for economic growth
item.description: The Africa Soft Power Summit is set to return with strong focus on how African countries can better connect finance, creativity, and human capital to drive long-term development. Now in its ninth edit...

item.title: Deputy minister defends actions after ethics breach in federal hiring case
item.description: A senior Canadian public servant, Christiane Fox, has defended her actions after the federal Ethics Commissioner found she breached conflict of interest rules by intervening in the hiring process of a...

item.title: NSW principal and three teachers charged with assaulting primary school boys in Queanbeyan
item.description: A principal and three teachers from a primary school in Queanbeyan, New South Wales, have been charged with assaulting three young boys aged between nine and 11. The incidents are alleged to have occu...

item.title: The Juans reflect on key Singapore performance before upcoming European tour
item.description: The Filipino pop band The Juans looked back on a significant milestone in their musical journey, describing their performance in Singapore as a “career-defining” experience. The group shared their ref...

item.title: PWD leaders urge President Ruto to improve inclusion and representation of persons with disabilities in national affairs
item.description: Hafid Maalim Ibrahim, the chairperson representing persons with disabilities (PWDs) in Northern Kenya, has written an open letter to President William Ruto expressing concern over what he terms contin...

item.title: Father kills eight children in domestic shooting incident in Louisiana
item.description: A very tragic incident happened in Shreveport, Louisiana, in the United States, where a man identified as Shamar Elkins killed eight children, including his own seven children and one more child, in a...

item.title: Economic dependence, political interests and global inertia slow shift away from fossil fuels
item.description: Duniya bhar mein 2023 ke COP28 summit ke baad yeh umeed paida hui thi ke fossil fuels se dheere dheere nikalna shuru ho jayega, lekin asal surat-e-haal kaafi mukhtalif hai. Aaj bhi global economy oil ...

item.title: Parents in tech launch petition calling for under-16 social media restrictions in Canada
item.description: A group of parents working in the tech industry has launched a national petition in Canada calling on the federal government to restrict access to social media for users under the age of 16. The initi...

item.title: Over 16,000 deaths recorded in 358 global disasters in 2025: CRED report
item.description: According to the Emergency Events Database maintained by the Centre for Research on the Epidemiology of Disasters (CRED), the year 2025 witnessed a significant rise in natural hazard-related disasters...

item.title: Remembering Cynthia Shange: A Legacy in Acting and Beauty Pageantry
item.description: The South African entertainment industry is mourning the loss of Cynthia Shange, a trailblazing actress and former Miss Black South Africa, who passed away at the age of 76 following an illness. Shang...

item.title: Tanzanian runners dominate Tanfoam Marathon in Arusha, secure multiple victories
item.description: Tanzanian athletes put up a strong and confident performance at the second edition of the Tanfoam Marathon held in Arusha, showing clear dominance in both the half marathon and full marathon categorie...

item.title: Australian surfer hospitalised after severe box jellyfish sting during Fiji surf trip
item.description: An Australian surfer has survived a potentially fatal encounter with a box jellyfish while on a surfing trip in Fiji, an incident that quickly escalated into a medical emergency. Guy Rowles was travel...

item.title: Shipping Remains Normal in Pakistan Amid Hormuz Tensions
item.description: Despite all the hullabaloo going on in the Strait of Hormuz – you know, all the tension – things are absolutely normal here in Pakistan when it comes to shipping, *alhumdulillah*. Oil tankers are stil...

item.title: Madlanga commission continues hearing testimony into alleged corruption and interference in South Africa’s criminal justice system
item.description: The Madlanga commission of inquiry, which is investigating allegations of criminal activity, political interference, and corruption within South Africa’s criminal justice system, continued its proceed...

item.title: Transport groups launch 3-day strike over rising fuel prices
item.description: Nagsimula ang panibagong tatlong araw na transport strike na ikinasa ng ilang transport groups bilang protesta sa patuloy na pagtaas ng presyo ng langis. Ayon sa ulat, ang pangunahing layunin ng kilos...

item.title: INEC clears its Chairman, says alleged X account linked to Amupitan is a case of digital impersonation
item.description: The Independent National Electoral Commission (INEC) has dismissed claims that its Chairman, Joash Amupitan, operates an X (formerly Twitter) account that made partisan comments, describing the allega...
